NCT01691521.snippet: Ortega HG et al. NEJM 2014;371:1198-1207 (MENSA). First anti-IL-5 Phase 3 vs placebo. Approved 2015.

evidence.label: Eligibility (AACT-derived)
evidence.text: AACT-derived (auto-populated 2026-05-04). Sex: ALL; min age 12 Years; max age 80 Years; healthy volunteers: f. CRITERIA: "Inclusion Criteria:~* Age. 12-80~* Documented physician-diagnosed asthma for at least 12 months~* Subjects who have received a physician-prescribed asthma controller medication with medium or high dose ICS for at least 12 months.~* Documented treatment with a total daily dose of either medium or high dose ICS (≥ 500 µg fluticasone propionate dry powder formulation equivalent total daily dose) for at least 3 months.~* At least one additional maintenance asthma controller medication is required a...

NMA_CONFIG.outcome_label: Annualised severe-asthma exacerbation rate — rate ratio scale
NMA_CONFIG.note: k=5 Pbo-anchored pivotal Phase 3 biologic trials. Star network. Tezepelumab (anti-TSLP) is the broadest phenotype — shows efficacy across eosinophilic + low-eosinophil subgroups. IL-5/5R agents (Mepo, Resliz, Benra) are eosinophil-specific. IL-4/13 (Dupi) covers T2-high.

title: RapidMeta Respiratory | Severe Eosinophilic/T2-High Asthma Biologics NMA v1.0
